questions.text: A Tsunami is a very large and powerful wave caused by ________ under the sea.
questions.explanation: Tsunamis are triggered by underwater seismic activity.

questions.text: On which date did the massive Tsunami hit Thailand and parts of India?
questions.explanation: The 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami occurred on December 26.

questions.text: Who was Ignesious in the chapter?
options: Manager of a cooperative society in Katchall
questions.explanation: Ignesious worked in Katchall, Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

questions.text: Why did Ignesious's wife wake him up at 6 a.m.?
questions.explanation: She felt the tremors of the earthquake that preceded the tsunami.

questions.text: How many of Ignesious's children were lost in the tsunami?
questions.explanation: Two of his children, along with his father-in-law and brother-in-law, were swept away.

questions.text: Who was Sanjeev?
questions.explanation: Sanjeev was a policeman serving in the Katchall island.

questions.text: Why did Sanjeev jump into the water after saving his own family?
options: To save the wife of John, the guest house cook
questions.explanation: He lost his life trying to rescue a neighbor in distress.

questions.text: How did 13-year-old Meghna survive the tsunami?
options: By holding onto a wooden door for two days
questions.explanation: Meghna floated on a wooden door and was eventually brought to shore by a wave.

questions.text: How many times did Meghna see relief helicopters overhead?
questions.explanation: She saw them 11 times, but they did not see her.

questions.text: Who was Almas Javed?
questions.explanation: Almas Javed was a young girl from Nancowry island.

questions.text: Where was Almas when she fainted during the tsunami?
questions.explanation: She climbed onto a floating log and later woke up in a hospital in Kamorta.

questions.text: Which country was Tilly Smith from?
questions.explanation: Tilly Smith was a British schoolgirl visiting Thailand.

questions.text: Where was Tilly Smith celebrating Christmas when the tsunami hit?
questions.explanation: Her family was on a beach in Phuket.

questions.text: What did Tilly notice about the sea that made her realize a tsunami was coming?
options: The sea started to rise, bubble, and form whirlpools
questions.explanation: She recognized the signs from a geography lesson she had attended in England.

questions.text: How did Tilly Smith learn about tsunamis?
options: From a geography lesson and a video at school
questions.explanation: She had seen a video of a tsunami in Hawaii (1946) in her class just two weeks before.

questions.text: Where did the Smith family and other tourists take refuge in the hotel?
questions.explanation: They ran to the third floor of the hotel, which withstood the impact.

questions.text: What is the belief mentioned about animals and the tsunami?
options: Animals are more afraid than humans
Animals have a 'sixth sense' and knew the disaster was coming
Animals were caused by the tsunami
questions.explanation: Few animals died compared to humans, suggesting they sensed the vibration early.

questions.text: How many people died in the Yala National Park in Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: 60 visitors were swept away, but only two water buffaloes died.

questions.text: What did the elephants at Yala National Park do before the tsunami?
questions.explanation: People observed elephants screaming and running away from Patanangala beach an hour before the waves hit.

questions.text: In the Galle coast of Sri Lanka, what did a gentleman's dogs do on the day of the tsunami?
options: They went for their usual run
They refused to go for their daily run on the beach
questions.explanation: By refusing to go out, they likely saved their owner's life.

questions.text: Tsunamis are also known as ________ waves.
questions.explanation: The word Tsunami comes from Japanese, meaning 'harbour waves'.

questions.text: Which of these islands is NOT part of the Andaman and Nicobar group mentioned?
questions.explanation: Phuket is in Thailand.

questions.text: Why did Almas's father rush everyone to higher ground?
options: He saw the sea water recede
questions.explanation: He knew that if the water receded, it would come back with great force.

questions.text: What happened to Almas's mother and aunts?
options: They were saved by a helicopter
They were swept away with the tree they were clinging to
questions.explanation: The tree was uprooted by the giant wave.

questions.text: Tilly's parents allowed her to leave the beach because:
options: She was hysterical and they realized something was wrong
They wanted to go to the hotel
questions.explanation: Tilly's extreme fear convinced her parents to take the threat seriously.

questions.text: What did the dogs in Galle usually enjoy?
questions.explanation: Their refusal to go out that day was highly unusual.

questions.text: The 2004 earthquake that caused the tsunami had its epicentre in:
questions.explanation: A massive earthquake occurred off the coast of Sumatra, Indonesia.

questions.text: Which animal is NOT mentioned as sensing the disaster?
questions.explanation: The text focuses on dogs, elephants, and flamingos.

questions.text: How many water buffaloes died in Yala National Park?
questions.explanation: While many visitors died, only two water buffaloes perished.

questions.text: What happened to Meghna when she reached the shore?
options: She was found walking in a daze
She was found by her parents
She was rescued by a boat
questions.explanation: She was found on the shore in a dazed state after being washed up by a wave.

questions.text: The geography lesson Tilly had was in which country?
questions.explanation: She had the lesson in her school in England before coming to Thailand.

questions.text: The 'relief helicopters' mentioned in Meghna's story were searching for:
questions.explanation: They were part of the rescue efforts after the disaster.

questions.text: In the story of Ignesious, what did he do with the television?
options: He put it on the floor to prevent it from falling
questions.explanation: He took it off the table carefully when he felt the tremors.

questions.text: Almas Javed was a student of ________ in Port Blair.
questions.explanation: She was a student of Carmel Convent.

questions.text: Tilly's sister was ________ years old.
questions.explanation: Tilly was 10 and her sister was 7.

questions.text: The hotel Tilly stayed in was called:
questions.explanation: The text mentions a 'hotel' but focuses on its location at Phuket.

questions.text: What is the main theme of the chapter 'The Tsunami'?
questions.explanation: The chapter covers the disaster, brave survival stories, and how awareness saved lives.

questions.text: How many visitors were swept away from the Patanangala beach in Yala National Park?
questions.explanation: 60 visitors died, which is a significant number compared to the animals.

questions.text: Meghna's parents and others were swept away along with her, but she was the only one to:
questions.explanation: The story highlights her miraculous survival alone for two days.

questions.text: The video Tilly saw in her geography class showed a tsunami in ________ in 1946.
questions.explanation: It was a video of the Hawaiian islands tsunami.
